parameters.query:
SELECT
    ca.id,
    ca.campaign_id,
    ca.email,
    MIN(ca.first_name) AS first_name,
    MIN(ca.last_name) AS last_name,
    SUM(ca.reply_count) AS reply_count,
    max(hb_lifecyclestage_check_timestamp) as hb_lifecyclestage_check_timestamp,
    CASE
        -- Check if there is a comma and handle the extraction first
        WHEN MIN(ca.linkedin_profile) LIKE '%,%' 
            THEN 
                -- Replace /sales/people/ with /in/ on the extracted part before the comma
                REPLACE(LEFT(MIN(ca.linkedin_profile), POSITION(',' IN MIN(ca.linkedin_profile)) - 1), '/sales/people/', '/in/')
        ELSE 
            -- For profiles without a comma, check for the replacement directly
            REPLACE(MIN(ca.linkedin_profile), '/sales/people/', '/in/')
    END AS linkedin_profile,
    MAX(ca.company_url) AS company_profile,
    -- Extracting domain from email to create the website column
    SUBSTRING(ca.email FROM POSITION('@' IN ca.email) + 1) AS website,
    c.created_at,
    c.updated_at,
    c.status,
    c.name
FROM
    outbound_activities.ce_campaign_activity ca
JOIN
    outbound_activities.ce_campaign c ON ca.campaign_id = c.campaign_id
--left join outbound_activities.hubspot hb on 

WHERE 
  hb_lifecyclestage_check_timestamp IS NULL 
  OR hb_lifecyclestage_check_timestamp < NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours'

  
GROUP BY
    ca.id,
    ca.campaign_id,
    ca.email,
    c.created_at,
    c.updated_at,
    c.status,
    c.name


limit 5000

nodes.notes: This postgres node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Postgres

parameters.query:
SELECT 
    h.campaign_id,
        c.status,
    c.name,
    COUNT(DISTINCT h.hubspot_company_id) AS total_companies,
    SUM(CASE WHEN h.lifecyclestage = 'lead' AND h.hs_num_open_deals != 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS lead_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN h.lifecyclestage = 'marketingqualifiedlead' AND h.hs_num_open_deals != 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS marketingqualifiedlead_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN h.lifecyclestage = 'salesqualifiedlead' AND h.hs_num_open_deals != 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS salesqualifiedlead_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN h.lifecyclestage = 'opportunity' AND h.hs_num_open_deals != 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS opportunity_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN h.lifecyclestage = 'customer' AND h.hs_num_open_deals != 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS customer_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN h.lifecyclestage = '140669943' AND h.hs_num_open_deals != 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS lifecyclestage_140669943_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN h.lifecyclestage = '140669942' AND h.hs_num_open_deals != 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS lifecyclestage_140669942_count
FROM 
    outbound_activities.hubspot h
JOIN 
    outbound_activities.ce_campaign c ON h.campaign_id = c.campaign_id
GROUP BY 
    h.campaign_id, c.status, c.name

nodes.notes: This postgres node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note4

parameters.content:
## Campaign Analytics Report Documentation

Overview

This report provides a high-level summary of campaign performance, designed to help stakeholders quickly assess the outcomes of marketing or sales campaigns. It includes data on campaign activity, targeted audiences, and progression metrics, allowing for a holistic view of campaign effectiveness.

## Key Metrics

	1	**Campaign Identification and Status**

        •	Campaign ID: A unique identifier assigned to each campaign for tracking purposes.
	•	Status: Indicates the current state of the campaign:
	•	Active: Campaign is ongoing.
	•	Paused: Campaign is temporarily on hold.
	•	Archived: Campaign has concluded.

2       **Targeting and Reach**

	•	Total Companies: Number of companies targeted within the campaign scope.


3	**Pipeline Metrics**

	•	Lead Count: Total number of leads generated by the campaign.
	•	Marketing Qualified Leads (MQLs): Leads that meet predefined marketing qualification criteria.
	•	Sales Qualified Leads (SQLs): Leads that are validated as sales-ready by the team.
	•	Opportunities: Potential deals created from campaign engagement.
	•	Customers: Number of deals successfully closed, converting leads into customers.


4	**Lifecycle Stages**

	•	Lifecycle Stage Metrics: Counts of entities (e.g., leads, opportunities, or customers) at specific lifecycle stages. These stages represent the journey from lead generation to conversion.



## How to Use This Report

	•	**Evaluate Campaign Success**: Compare metrics like total companies, leads, and customers to understand campaign impact.
	•	**Understand Pipeline Health**: Analyze how many entities progress through the funnel (e.g., from MQL to SQL to Opportunity).
	•	**Monitor Campaign Status**: Use the status column to focus on active campaigns or review the outcomes of archived ones.
	•	**Assess Engagement**: Check opportunity and customer counts to gauge how effective the campaign is in driving conversions.

parameters.query:
SELECT 
    h.campaign_id,
        c.status,
    c.name,
    COUNT(DISTINCT h.hubspot_company_id) AS total_companies,
    SUM(CASE WHEN h.lifecyclestage = 'lead' AND h.hs_num_open_deals != 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS lead_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN h.lifecyclestage = 'marketingqualifiedlead' AND h.hs_num_open_deals != 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS marketingqualifiedlead_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN h.lifecyclestage = 'salesqualifiedlead' AND h.hs_num_open_deals != 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS salesqualifiedlead_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN h.lifecyclestage = 'opportunity' AND h.hs_num_open_deals != 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS opportunity_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN h.lifecyclestage = 'customer' AND h.hs_num_open_deals != 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS customer_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN h.lifecyclestage = '140669943' AND h.hs_num_open_deals != 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS lifecyclestage_140669943_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN h.lifecyclestage = '140669942' AND h.hs_num_open_deals != 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS lifecyclestage_140669942_count
FROM 
    outbound_activities.hubspot h
JOIN 
    outbound_activities.ce_campaign c ON h.campaign_id = c.campaign_id
GROUP BY 
    h.campaign_id, c.status, c.name

SELECT
    ca.id,
    ca.campaign_id,
    ca.email,
    MIN(ca.first_name) AS first_name,
    MIN(ca.last_name) AS last_name,
    SUM(ca.reply_count) AS reply_count,
    max(hb_lifecyclestage_check_timestamp) as hb_lifecyclestage_check_timestamp,
    CASE
        -- Check if there is a comma and handle the extraction first
        WHEN MIN(ca.linkedin_profile) LIKE '%,%' 
            THEN 
                -- Replace /sales/people/ with /in/ on the extracted part before the comma
                REPLACE(LEFT(MIN(ca.linkedin_profile), POSITION(',' IN MIN(ca.linkedin_profile)) - 1), '/sales/people/', '/in/')
        ELSE 
            -- For profiles without a comma, check for the replacement directly
            REPLACE(MIN(ca.linkedin_profile), '/sales/people/', '/in/')
    END AS linkedin_profile,
    MAX(ca.company_url) AS company_profile,
    -- Extracting domain from email to create the website column
    SUBSTRING(ca.email FROM POSITION('@' IN ca.email) + 1) AS website,
    c.created_at,
    c.updated_at,
    c.status,
    c.name
FROM
    outbound_activities.ce_campaign_activity ca
JOIN
    outbound_activities.ce_campaign c ON ca.campaign_id = c.campaign_id
--left join outbound_activities.hubspot hb on 

WHERE 
  hb_lifecyclestage_check_timestamp IS NULL 
  OR hb_lifecyclestage_check_timestamp < NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours'

  
GROUP BY
    ca.id,
    ca.campaign_id,
    ca.email,
    c.created_at,
    c.updated_at,
    c.status,
    c.name


limit 5000

## Key Metrics

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: hubspot  O Auth2  A P I details
google Sheets  O Auth2  A P I details
configurationSteps: Open the scheduler node "Schedule Trigger".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Open the "EXTRACT CAMPAIGN DATA" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "FETCH ALL CAMPAIGNS" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Loop Over Items" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "UPDATE CAMPAIGN" node (n8n-nodes-base.postgres) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Merge" node (n8n-nodes-base.merge) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Loop Over Items1" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "HubSpot" node (n8n-nodes-base.hubspot) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "If Node" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "UPSERT CAMPAIGN ACTIVITY" node (n8n-nodes-base.postgres) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "HUBSPOT TABLE" node (n8n-nodes-base.postgres) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "SEARCH" node (n8n-nodes-base.postgres) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Postgres1" node (n8n-nodes-base.postgres) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "UPDATE HUBSPOT ACTIVITY TABLE" node (n8n-nodes-base.postgres) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Postgres" node (n8n-nodes-base.postgres) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Google Sheets" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "SET SMARTLEAD API KEY".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
